name: Sonny Criss
abstract: William "Sonny" Criss (23 October 1927 – 19 November 1977) was an American jazz musician. An alto saxophonist of prominence during the bebop era of jazz, he was one of many players influenced by Charlie Parker.

about_quotes.speaker: Howard McGhee
about_quotes.evidence: Sonny Criss, Teddy Edwards, Gene Montgomery and Bird. So the bad was beautiful, but we weren't making any money.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard

about_quotes.speaker: Buddy Collette
about_quotes.evidence: So a couple of others are James Newton, the flutist, and Big Jim McNeely, Sonny Criss, let’s see the other names, John Stevens, I don’t know if you know him, he’s a fine musician here in L.A.
about_quotes.doc_title: Buddy Collette interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 13, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Wilder
about_quotes.evidence: Sonny Criss was with Basie. His father was nicknamed Christopher Columbus. He was a great drummer. He used to play at Minton’s nightclub.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Wilder — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
